\topts[\"THIS_INTERFACE_MAC_2\"] = uint64(sliceToBe16(mac[4:6]))\n194: \n195: \tifIndex := uint32(iface.Attrs().Index)\n196: \n197: \tif !option.Config.EnableHostLegacyRouting {\n198: \t\topts[\"SECCTX_FROM_IPCACHE\"] = uint64(secctxFromIpcacheEnabled)\n199: \t} else {\n200: \t\topts[\"SECCTX_FROM_IPCACHE\"] = uint64(secctxFromIpcacheDisabled)\n201: \t}\n202: \n203: \t// Overwrite the host endpoint ifindex assigned in ELFVariableSubstitutions\n204: \t// with the external device's.\n205: \topts[\"interface_ifindex\"] = uint64(ifIndex)\n206: \n207: \tif option.Config.EnableBPFMasquerade && ifName != defaults.SecondHostDevice {\n208: \t\tipv4, ipv6 := bpfMasqAddrs(ifName, cfg)\n209: \n210: \t\tif option.Config.EnableIPv4Masquerade && ipv4.IsValid() {\n211: \t\t\topts[\"IPV4_MASQUERADE\"] = uint64(byteorder.NetIPv4ToHost32(ipv4.AsSlice()))\n212: \t\t}\n213: \t\tif option.Config.EnableIPv6Masquerade && ipv6.IsValid() {\n214: \t\t\tipv6Bytes := ipv6.AsSlice()\n215: \t\t\topts[\"IPV6_MASQUERADE_1\"] = sliceToBe64(ipv6Bytes[0:8])\n216: \t\t\topts[\"IPV6_MASQUERADE_2\"] = sliceToBe64(ipv6Bytes[8:16])\n217: \t\t}\n218: \t}\n219: \n220: \tcallsMapHostDevice := bpf.LocalMapName(callsmap.HostMapName, templateLxcID)\n221: \tstrings[callsMapHostDevice] = bpf.LocalMapName(callsmap.NetdevMapName, uint16(ifIndex))\n222: \n223: \treturn opts, strings, nil\n224: }\n225: \n226: func isObsoleteDev(dev string, devices []string) bool {\n227: \t// exclude devices we never attach to/from_netdev to.\n228: \tfor _, prefix := range defaults.ExcludedDevicePrefixes {\n229: \t\tif strings.HasPrefix(dev, prefix) {\n230: \t\t\treturn false\n231: \t\t}\n232: \t}\n233: \n234: \t// exclude devices that will still be managed going forward.\n235: \tfor _, d := range devices {\n236: \t\tif dev == d {\n237: \t\t\treturn false\n238: \t\t}\n239: \t}\n240: \n241: \treturn true\n242: }\n243: \n244: // removeObsoleteNetdevPrograms removes cil_to_netdev and cil_from_netdev from devices\n245: // that cilium potentially doesn't manage anymore after a restart, e.g. if the set of\n246: // devices changes between restarts.\n247: //\n248: // This code assumes that the agent was upgraded from a prior version while maintaining\n249: // the same list of managed physical devices. This ensures that all tc bpf filters get\n250: // replaced using the naming convention of the 'current' agent build. For example,\n251: // before 1.13, most filters were named e.g. bpf_host.o:[to-host], to be changed to\n252: // cilium-<device> in 1.13, then to cil_to_host-<device> in 1.14. As a result, this\n253: // function only cleans up filters following the current naming scheme.\n254: func removeObsoleteNetdevPrograms(devices []string) error {\n255: \tlinks, err := safenetlink.LinkList()\n256: \tif err != nil {\n257: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"retrieving all netlink devices: %w\", err)\n258: \t}\n259: \n260: \t// collect all devices that have netdev programs attached on either ingress or egress.\n261: \tingressDevs := []netlink.Link{}\n262: \tegressDevs := []netlink.Link{}\n263: \tfor _, l := range links {\n264: \t\tif !isObsoleteDev(l.Attrs().Name, devices) {\n265: \t\t\tcontinue\n266: \t\t}\n267: \n268: \t\t// Remove the per-device bpffs directory containing pinned links and\n269: \t\t// per-endpoint maps.\n270: \t\tbpffsPath := bpffsDeviceDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), l)\n271: \t\tif err := bpf.Remove(bpffsPath); err != nil {\n272: \t\t\tlog.WithError(err).WithField(logfields.Device, l.Attrs().Name)\n273: \t\t}\n274: \n275: \t\tingressFilters, err := safenetlink.FilterList(l, directionToParent(dirIngress))\n276: \t\tif err != nil {\n277: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"listing ingress filters: %w\", err)\n278: \t\t}\n279: \t\tfor _, filter := range ingressFilters {\n280: \t\t\tif bpfFilter, ok := filter.(*netlink.BpfFilter); ok {\n281: \t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(bpfFilter.Name, symbolFromHostNetdevEp) {\n282: \t\t\t\t\tingressDevs = append(ingressDevs, l)\n283: \t\t\t\t}\n284: \t\t\t}\n285: \t\t}\n286: \n287: \t\tegressFilters, err := safenetlink.FilterList(l, directionToParent(dirEgress))\n288: \t\tif err != nil {\n289: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"listing egress filters: %w\", err)\n290: \t\t}\n291: \t\tfor _, filter := range egressFilters {\n292: \t\t\tif bpfFilter, ok := filter.(*netlink.BpfFilter); ok {\n293: \t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(bpfFilter.Name, symbolToHostNetdevEp) {\n294: \t\t\t\t\tegressDevs = append(egressDevs, l)\n295: \t\t\t\t}\n296: \t\t\t}\n297: \t\t}\n298: \t}\n299: \n300: \tfor _, dev := range ingressDevs {\n301: \t\terr = removeTCFilters(dev, directionToParent(dirIngress))\n302: \t\tif err != nil {\n303: \t\t\tlog.WithError(err).Errorf(\"couldn't remove ingress tc filters from %s\", dev.Attrs().Name)\n304: \t\t}\n305: \t}\n306: \n307: \tfor _, dev := range egressDevs {\n308: \t\terr = removeTCFilters(dev, directionToParent(dirEgress))\n309: \t\tif err != nil {\n310: \t\t\tlog.WithError(err).Errorf(\"couldn't remove egress tc filters from %s\", dev.Attrs().Name)\n311: \t\t}\n312: \t}\n313: \n314: \treturn nil\n315: }\n316: \n317: // reloadHostEndpoint (re)attaches programs from bpf_host.c to cilium_host,\n318: // cilium_net and external (native) devices.\n319: func reloadHostEndpoint(cfg *datapath.LocalNodeConfiguration, ep datapath.Endpoint, spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec) error {\n320: \t// Replace programs on cilium_host.\n321: \tif err := attachCiliumHost(ep, spec); err != nil {\n322: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"attaching cilium_host: %w\", err)\n323: \t}\n324: \n325: \tif err := attachCiliumNet(cfg, ep, spec); err != nil {\n326: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"attaching cilium_host: %w\", err)\n327: \t}\n328: \n329: \tif err := attachNetworkDevices(cfg, ep, spec); err != nil {\n330: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"attaching cilium_host: %w\", err)\n331: \t}\n332: \n333: \treturn nil\n334: }\n335: \n336: // attachCiliumHost inserts the host endpoint's policy program into the global\n337: // cilium_call_policy map and attaches programs from bpf_host.c to cilium_host.\n338: func attachCiliumHost(ep datapath.Endpoint, spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec) error {\n339: \thost, err := safenetlink.LinkByName(ep.InterfaceName())\n340: \tif err != nil {\n341: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"retrieving device %s: %w\", ep.InterfaceName(), err)\n342: \t}\n343: \n344: \tvar hostObj hostObjects\n345: \tcommit, err := bpf.LoadAndAssign(&hostObj, spec, &bpf.CollectionOptions{\n346: \t\tCollectionOptions: ebpf.CollectionOptions{\n347: \t\t\tMaps: ebpf.MapOptions{PinPath: bpf.TCGlobalsPath()},\n348: \t\t},\n349: \t\tMapRenames: ELFMapSubstitutions(ep),\n350: \t\tConstants:  ELFVariableSubstitutions(ep),\n351: \t})\n352: \tif err != nil {\n353: \t\treturn err\n354: \t}\n355: \tdefer hostObj.Close()\n356: \n357: \t// Insert host endpoint policy program.\n358: \tif err := hostObj.PolicyMap.Update(uint32(ep.GetID()), hostObj.PolicyProg, ebpf.UpdateAny); err != nil {\n359: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"inserting host endpoint policy program: %w\", err)\n360: \t}\n361: \n362: \t// Attach cil_to_host to cilium_host ingress.\n363: \tif err := attachSKBProgram(host, hostObj.ToHost, symbolToHostEp,\n364: \t\tbpffsDeviceLinksDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), host), netlink.HANDLE_MIN_INGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n365: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s ingress: %w\", ep.InterfaceName(), err)\n366: \t}\n367: \t// Attach cil_from_host to cilium_host egress.\n368: \tif err := attachSKBProgram(host, hostObj.FromHost, symbolFromHostEp,\n369: \t\tbpffsDeviceLinksDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), host), netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n370: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s egress: %w\", ep.InterfaceName(), err)\n371: \t}\n372: \n373: \tif err := commit(); err != nil {\n374: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"committing bpf pins: %w\", err)\n375: \t}\n376: \n377: \treturn nil\n378: }\n379: \n380: // attachCiliumNet attaches programs from bpf_host.c to cilium_net.\n381: func attachCiliumNet(cfg *datapath.LocalNodeConfiguration, ep datapath.Endpoint, spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec) error {\n382: \tnet, err := safenetlink.LinkByName(defaults.SecondHostDevice)\n383: \tif err != nil {\n384: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"retrieving device %s: %w\", defaults.SecondHostDevice, err)\n385: \t}\n386: \n387: \tconsts, renames, err := hostRewrites(cfg, ep, defaults.SecondHostDevice)\n388: \tif err != nil {\n389: \t\treturn err\n390: \t}\n391: \n392: \tvar netObj hostNetObjects\n393: \tcommit, err := bpf.LoadAndAssign(&netObj, spec, &bpf.CollectionOptions{\n394: \t\tCollectionOptions: ebpf.CollectionOptions{\n395: \t\t\tMaps: ebpf.MapOptions{PinPath: bpf.TCGlobalsPath()},\n396: \t\t},\n397: \t\tMapRenames: renames,\n398: \t\tConstants:  consts,\n399: \t})\n400: \tif err != nil {\n401: \t\treturn err\n402: \t}\n403: \tdefer netObj.Close()\n404: \n405: \t// Attach cil_to_host to cilium_net.\n406: \tif err := attachSKBProgram(net, netObj.ToHost, symbolToHostEp,\n407: \t\tbpffsDeviceLinksDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), net), netlink.HANDLE_MIN_INGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n408: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s ingress: %w\", defaults.SecondHostDevice, err)\n409: \t}\n410: \n411: \tif err := commit(); err != nil {\n412: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"committing bpf pins: %w\", err)\n413: \t}\n414: \n415: \treturn nil\n416: }\n417: \n418: // attachNetworkDevices attaches programs from bpf_host.c to externally-facing\n419: // devices and the wireguard device. Attaches cil_from_netdev to ingress and\n420: // optionally cil_to_netdev to egress if enabled features require it.\n421: func attachNetworkDevices(cfg *datapath.LocalNodeConfiguration, ep datapath.Endpoint, spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec) error {\n422: \tdevices := cfg.DeviceNames()\n423: \n424: \t// Selectively attach bpf_host to cilium_wg0.\n425: \tif option.Config.NeedBPFHostOnWireGuardDevice() {\n426: \t\tdevices = append(devices, wgTypes.IfaceName)\n427: \t}\n428: \n429: \t// Replace programs on physical devices, ignoring devices that don't exist.\n430: \tfor _, device := range devices {\n431: \t\tiface, err := safenetlink.LinkByName(device)\n432: \t\tif err != nil {\n433: \t\t\tlog.WithError(err).WithField(\"device\", device).Warn(\"Link does not exist\")\n434: \t\t\tcontinue\n435: \t\t}\n436: \n437: \t\tlinkDir := bpffsDeviceLinksDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), iface)\n438: \n439: \t\tconsts, renames, err := hostRewrites(cfg, ep, device)\n440: \t\tif err != nil {\n441: \t\t\treturn err\n442: \t\t}\n443: \n444: \t\tvar netdevObj hostNetdevObjects\n445: \t\tcommit, err := bpf.LoadAndAssign(&netdevObj, spec, &bpf.CollectionOptions{\n446: \t\t\tCollectionOptions: ebpf.CollectionOptions{\n447: \t\t\t\tMaps: ebpf.MapOptions{PinPath: bpf.TCGlobalsPath()},\n448: \t\t\t},\n449: \t\t\tMapRenames: renames,\n450: \t\t\tConstants:  consts,\n451: \t\t})\n452: \t\tif err != nil {\n453: \t\t\treturn err\n454: \t\t}\n455: \t\tdefer netdevObj.Close()\n456: \n457: \t\t// Attach cil_from_netdev to ingress.\n458: \t\tif err := attachSKBProgram(iface, netdevObj.FromNetdev, symbolFromHostNetdevEp,\n459: \t\t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_INGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n460: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s ingress: %w\", device, err)\n461: \t\t}\n462: \n463: \t\t// When downgrading from v1.18, upon successfully attaching cil_from_netdev\n464: \t\t// to cilium_wg0, we must remove the cil_from_wireguard program.\n465: \t\tif device == wgTypes.IfaceName {\n466: \t\t\tif err := detachSKBProgram(iface, symbolFromWireguard,\n467: \t\t\t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_INGRESS); err != nil {\n468: \t\t\t\tlog.WithField(\"device\", device).Error(err)\n469: \t\t\t}\n470: \t\t\t// Cleanup also calls map from v1.18 after downgrade.\n471: \t\t\tcleanCallsMaps(fmt.Sprintf(\"cilium_calls_wireguard_%d\", iface.Attrs().Index))\n472: \t\t}\n473: \n474: \t\tif option.Config.AreDevicesRequired() {\n475: \t\t\t// Attaching bpf_host to cilium_wg0 is required for encrypting KPR\n476: \t\t\t// traffic. Only ingress prog (aka \"from-netdev\") is needed to handle\n477: \t\t\t// the rev-NAT xlations.\n478: \t\t\tif device != wgTypes.IfaceName {\n479: \t\t\t\t// Attach cil_to_netdev to egress.\n480: \t\t\t\tif err := attachSKBProgram(iface, netdevObj.ToNetdev, symbolToHostNetdevEp,\n481: \t\t\t\t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n482: \t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s egress: %w\", device, err)\n483: \t\t\t\t}\n484: \t\t\t}\n485: \t\t} else {\n486: \t\t\t// Remove any previously attached device from egress path if BPF\n487: \t\t\t// NodePort and host firewall are disabled.\n488: \t\t\tif err := detachSKBProgram(iface, symbolToHostNetdevEp, linkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS); err != nil {\n489: \t\t\t\tlog.WithField(\"device\", device).Error(err)\n490: \t\t\t}\n491: \t\t}\n492: \n493: \t\tif err := commit(); err != nil {\n494: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"committing bpf pins: %w\", err)\n495: \t\t}\n496: \t}\n497: \n498: \t// Call immediately after attaching programs to make it obvious that a\n499: \t// program was wrongfully detached due to a bug or misconfiguration.\n500: \tif err := removeObsoleteNetdevPrograms(devices); err != nil {\n501: \t\tlog.WithError(err).Error(\"Failed to remove obsolete netdev programs\")\n502: \t}\n503: \n504: \treturn nil\n505: }\n506: \n507: // reloadEndpoint loads programs in spec into the device used by ep.\n508: //\n509: // spec is modified by the method and it is the callers responsibility to copy\n510: // it if necessary.\n511: func reloadEndpoint(ep datapath.Endpoint, spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec) error {\n512: \tdevice := ep.InterfaceName()\n513: \n514: \tvar obj lxcObjects\n515: \tcommit, err := bpf.LoadAndAssign(&obj, spec, &bpf.CollectionOptions{\n516: \t\tCollectionOptions: ebpf.CollectionOptions{\n517: \t\t\tMaps: ebpf.MapOptions{PinPath: bpf.TCGlobalsPath()},\n518: \t\t},\n519: \t\tMapRenames: ELFMapSubstitutions(ep),\n520: \t\tConstants:  ELFVariableSubstitutions(ep),\n521: \t})\n522: \tif err != nil {\n523: \t\treturn err\n524: \t}\n525: \tdefer obj.Close()\n526: \n527: \t// Insert policy programs before attaching entrypoints to tc hooks.\n528: \t// Inserting a policy program is considered an attachment, since it makes\n529: \t// the code reachable by bpf_host when it evaluates policy for the endpoint.\n530: \t// All internal tail call plumbing needs to be done before this point.\n531: \t// If the agent dies uncleanly after the first program has been inserted,\n532: \t// the endpoint's connectivity will be partially broken or exhibit undefined\n533: \t// behaviour like missed tail calls or drops.\n534: \tif err := obj.PolicyMap.Update(uint32(ep.GetID()), obj.PolicyProg, ebpf.UpdateAny); err != nil {\n535: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"inserting endpoint policy program: %w\", err)\n536: \t}\n537: \tif err := obj.EgressPolicyMap.Update(uint32(ep.GetID()), obj.EgressPolicyProg, ebpf.UpdateAny); err != nil {\n538: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"inserting endpoint egress policy program: %w\", err)\n539: \t}\n540: \n541: \tiface, err := safenetlink.LinkByName(device)\n542: \tif err != nil {\n543: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"retrieving device %s: %w\", device, err)\n544: \t}\n545: \n546: \tlinkDir := bpffsEndpointLinksDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), ep)\n547: \tif err := attachSKBProgram(iface, obj.FromContainer, symbolFromEndpoint,\n548: \t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_INGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n549: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s ingress: %w\", device, err)\n550: \t}\n551: \n552: \tif ep.RequireEgressProg() {\n553: \t\tif err := attachSKBProgram(iface, obj.ToContainer, symbolToEndpoint,\n554: \t\t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n555: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s egress: %w\", device, err)\n556: \t\t}\n557: \t} else {\n558: \t\tif err := detachSKBProgram(iface, symbolToEndpoint, linkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS); err != nil {\n559: \t\t\tlog.WithField(\"device\", device).Error(err)\n560: \t\t}\n561: \t}\n562: \n563: \tif err := commit(); err != nil {\n564: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"committing bpf pins: %w\", err)\n565: \t}\n566: \n567: \tif ep.RequireEndpointRoute() {\n568: \t\tscopedLog := ep.Logger(subsystem).WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n569: \t\t\tlogfields.Interface: device,\n570: \t\t})\n571: \t\tif ip := ep.IPv4Address(); ip.IsValid() {\n572: \t\t\tif err := upsertEndpointRoute(ep, *netipx.AddrIPNet(ip)); err != nil {\n573: \t\t\t\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\"Failed to upsert route\")\n574: \t\t\t}\n575: \t\t}\n576: \t\tif ip := ep.IPv6Address(); ip.IsValid() {\n577: \t\t\tif err := upsertEndpointRoute(ep, *netipx.AddrIPNet(ip)); err != nil {\n578: \t\t\t\tscopedLog.WithError(err).Warn(\"Failed to upsert route\")\n579: \t\t\t}\n580: \t\t}\n581: \t}\n582: \n583: \treturn nil\n584: }\n585: \n586: func replaceOverlayDatapath(ctx context.Context, cArgs []string, device netlink.Link) error {\n587: \tif err := compileOverlay(ctx, cArgs); err != nil {\n588: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"compiling overlay program: %w\", err)\n589: \t}\n590: \n591: \tspec, err := bpf.LoadCollectionSpec(overlayObj)\n592: \tif err != nil {\n593: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"loading eBPF ELF %s: %w\", overlayObj, err)\n594: \t}\n595: \n596: \tvar obj overlayObjects\n597: \tcommit, err := bpf.LoadAndAssign(&obj, spec, &bpf.CollectionOptions{\n598: \t\tConstants: map[string]uint64{\n599: \t\t\t\"interface_ifindex\": uint64(device.Attrs().Index),\n600: \t\t},\n601: \t\tCollectionOptions: ebpf.CollectionOptions{\n602: \t\t\tMaps: ebpf.MapOptions{PinPath: bpf.TCGlobalsPath()},\n603: \t\t},\n604: \t})\n605: \tif err != nil {\n606: \t\treturn err\n607: \t}\n608: \tdefer obj.Close()\n609: \n610: \tlinkDir := bpffsDeviceLinksDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), device)\n611: \tif err := attachSKBProgram(device, obj.FromOverlay, symbolFromOverlay,\n612: \t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_INGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n613: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s ingress: %w\", device, err)\n614: \t}\n615: \tif err := attachSKBProgram(device, obj.ToOverlay, symbolToOverlay,\n616: \t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n617: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s egress: %w\", device, err)\n618: \t}\n619: \n620: \tif err := commit(); err != nil {\n621: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"committing bpf pins: %w\", err)\n622: \t}\n623: \n624: \treturn nil\n625: }\n626: \n627: func replaceWireguardDatapath(ctx context.Context, cArgs []string, device netlink.Link) (err error) {\n628: \tif err := compileWireguard(ctx, cArgs); err != nil {\n629: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"compiling wireguard program: %w\", err)\n630: \t}\n631: \n632: \tspec, err := bpf.LoadCollectionSpec(wireguardObj)\n633: \tif err != nil {\n634: \t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"loading eBPF ELF %s: %w\", wireguardObj, err)\n635: \t}\n636: \n637: \tvar obj wireguardObjects\n638: \tcommit, err := bpf.LoadAndAssign(&obj, spec, &bpf.CollectionOptions{\n639: \t\tConstants: map[string]uint64{\n640: \t\t\t\"interface_ifindex\": uint64(device.Attrs().Index),\n641: \t\t},\n642: \t\tCollectionOptions: ebpf.CollectionOptions{\n643: \t\t\tMaps: ebpf.MapOptions{PinPath: bpf.TCGlobalsPath()},\n644: \t\t},\n645: \t})\n646: \tif err != nil {\n647: \t\treturn err\n648: \t}\n649: \tdefer obj.Close()\n650: \n651: \tlinkDir := bpffsDeviceLinksDir(bpf.CiliumPath(), device)\n652: \t// Attach/detach cil_to_wireguard to/from egress.\n653: \tif option.Config.NeedEgressOnWireGuardDevice() {\n654: \t\tif err := attachSKBProgram(device, obj.ToWireguard, symbolToWireguard,\n655: \t\t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS, option.Config.EnableTCX); err != nil {\n656: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"interface %s egress: %w\", device, err)\n657: \t\t}\n658: \t} else {\n659: \t\tif err := detachSKBProgram(device, symbolToWireguard,\n660: \t\t\tlinkDir, netlink.HANDLE_MIN_EGRESS); 32: // outputType determines the type to be generated by the compilation steps.
33: type outputType string
34: 
35: const (
36: 	outputObject = outputType("obj")
37: 	outputSource = outputType("c")
38: 
39: 	compiler = "clang"
40: 
41: 	endpointPrefix = "bpf_lxc"
42: 	endpointProg   = endpointPrefix + "." + string(outputSource)
43: 	endpointObj    = endpointPrefix + ".o"
44: 
45: 	hostEndpointPrefix       = "bpf_host"
46: 	hostEndpointNetdevPrefix = "bpf_netdev_"
47: 	hostEndpointProg         = hostEndpointPrefix + "." + string(outputSource)
48: 	hostEndpointObj          = hostEndpointPrefix + ".o"
49: 
50: 	networkPrefix = "bpf_network"
51: 	networkProg   = networkPrefix + "." + string(outputSource)
52: 	networkObj    = networkPrefix + ".o"
53: 
54: 	xdpPrefix = "bpf_xdp"
55: 	xdpProg   = xdpPrefix + "." + string(outputSource)
56: 	xdpObj    = xdpPrefix + ".o"
57: 
58: 	overlayPrefix = "bpf_overlay"
59: 	overlayProg   = overlayPrefix + "." + string(outputSource)
60: 	overlayObj    = overlayPrefix + ".o"
61: 
62: 	wireguardPrefix = "bpf_wireguard"
63: 	wireguardProg   = wireguardPrefix + "." + string(outputSource)
64: 	wireguardObj    = wireguardPrefix + ".o"
65: )

67: var (
68: 	probeCPUOnce sync.Once
69: 
70: 	// default fallback
71: 	nameBPFCPU = "v1"
72: )

74: // progInfo describes a program to be compiled with the expected output format
75: type progInfo struct {
76: 	// Source is the program source (base) filename to be compiled
77: 	Source string
78: 	// Output is the expected (base) filename produced from the source
79: 	Output string
80: 	// OutputType to be created by LLVM
81: 	OutputType outputType
82: 	// Options are passed directly to LLVM as individual parameters
83: 	Options []string
84: }

86: func (pi *progInfo) AbsoluteOutput(dir *directoryInfo) string {
87: 	return filepath.Join(dir.Output, pi.Output)
88: }

93: 	Library string

95: 	Runtime string
96: 	// State contains node, lxc, and features headers for templatization
97: 	State string
98: 	// Output is the directory where the files will be stored
99: 	Output string

103: 	standardCFlags = []string{"-O2", "--target=bpf", "-std=gnu89",
104: 		"-nostdinc",
105: 		"-Wall", "-Wextra", "-Werror", "-Wshadow",
106: 		"-Wno-address-of-packed-member",
107: 		"-Wno-unknown-warning-option",
108: 		"-Wno-gnu-variable-sized-type-not-at-end",
109: 		"-Wdeclaration-after-statement",
110: 		"-Wimplicit-int-conversion",
111: 		"-Wenum-conversion"}

113: 	// testIncludes allows the unit tests to inject additional include
114: 	// paths into the compile command at test time. It is usually nil.
115: 	testIncludes []string
116: 
117: 	epProg = &progInfo{
118: 		Source:     endpointProg,
119: 		Output:     endpointObj,
120: 		OutputType: outputObject,
121: 	}

134: // getBPFCPU returns the BPF CPU for this host.
135: func getBPFCPU() string {
136: 	probeCPUOnce.Do(func() {
137: 		if !option.Config.DryMode {
138: 			// We can probe the availability of BPF instructions indirectly
139: 			// based on what kernel helpers are available when both were
140: 			// added in the same release.
141: 			// We want to enable v3 only on kernels 5.10+ where we have
142: 			// tested it and need it to work around complexity issues.
143: 			if probes.HaveV3ISA() == nil {
144: 				if probes.HaveProgramHelper(ebpf.SchedCLS, asm.FnRedirectNeigh) == nil {
145: 					nameBPFCPU = "v3"
146: 					return
147: 				}
148: 			}
149: 			// We want to enable v2 on all kernels that support it, that is,
150: 			// kernels 4.14+.
151: 			if probes.HaveV2ISA() == nil {
152: 				nameBPFCPU = "v2"
153: 			}
154: 		}
155: 	})
156: 	return nameBPFCPU
157: }
158: 
159: func pidFromProcess(proc *os.Process) string {
160: 	result := "not-started"
161: 	if proc != nil {
162: 		result = fmt.Sprintf("%d", proc.Pid)
163: 	}
164: 	return result
165: }

167: // compile and optionally link a program.
168: //
169: // May output assembly or source code after prepocessing.
170: func compile(ctx context.Context, prog *progInfo, dir *directoryInfo) (string, error) {
171: 	possibleCPUs, err := ebpf.PossibleCPU()
172: 	if err != nil {
173: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get number of possible CPUs: %w", err)
174: 	}
175: 
176: 	compileArgs := append(testIncludes,
177: 		fmt.Sprintf("-I%s", path.Join(dir.Runtime, "globals")),
178: 		fmt.Sprintf("-I%s", dir.State),
179: 		fmt.Sprintf("-I%s", dir.Library),
180: 		fmt.Sprintf("-I%s", path.Join(dir.Library, "include")),
181: 	)
182: 
183: 	switch prog.OutputType {
184: 	case outputSource:
185: 		compileArgs = append(compileArgs, "-E") // Preprocessor
186: 	case outputObject:
187: 		compileArgs = append(compileArgs, "-g")
188: 	}
189: 
190: 	compileArgs = append(compileArgs, standardCFlags...)
191: 	compileArgs = append(compileArgs, fmt.Sprintf("-D__NR_CPUS__=%d", possibleCPUs))
192: 	compileArgs = append(compileArgs, "-mcpu="+getBPFCPU())
193: 	compileArgs = append(compileArgs, prog.Options...)
194: 	compileArgs = append(compileArgs,
195: 		"-c", path.Join(dir.Library, prog.Source),
196: 		"-o", "-", // Always output to stdout
197: 	)
198: 
199: 	log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
200: 		"target": compiler,
201: 		"args":   compileArgs,
202: 	}).Debug("Launching compiler")
203: 
204: 	compileCmd, cancelCompile := exec.WithCancel(ctx, compiler, compileArgs...)
205: 	defer cancelCompile()
206: 
207: 	output, err := os.Create(prog.AbsoluteOutput(dir))
208: 	if err != nil {
209: 		return "", err
210: 	}
211: 	defer output.Close()
212: 	compileCmd.Stdout = output
213: 
214: 	var compilerStderr bytes.Buffer
215: 	compileCmd.Stderr = &compilerStderr
216: 
217: 	if err := compileCmd.Run(); err != nil {
218: 		err = fmt.Errorf("Failed to compile %s: %w", prog.Output, err)
219: 
220: 		// In linux/unix based implementations, cancelling the context for a cmd.Run() will
221: 		// return errors: "context cancelled" if the context is cancelled prior to the process
222: 		// starting and "signal: killed" if it is already running.
223: 		// This can mess up calling logging logic which expects the returned error to have
224: 		// context.Cancelled so we join this error in to fix that.
225: 		if errors.Is(ctx.Err(), context.Canceled) &&
226: 			compileCmd.ProcessState != nil &&
227: 			!compileCmd.ProcessState.Exited() &&
228: 			strings.HasSuffix(err.Error(), syscall.SIGKILL.String()) {
229: 			err = errors.Join(err, ctx.Err())
230: 		}
231: 
232: 		if !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
233: 			log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
234: 				"compiler-pid": pidFromProcess(compileCmd.Process),
235: 			}).Error(err)
236: 		}
237: 
238: 		scanner := bufio.NewScanner(io.LimitReader(&compilerStderr, 1_000_000))
239: 		for scanner.Scan() {
240: 			log.Warn(scanner.Text())
241: 		}
242: 
243: 		return "", err
244: 	}
245: 
246: 	// Cmd.ProcessState is populated by Cmd.Wait(). Cmd.Run() bails out if
247: 	// Cmd.Start() fails, which will leave Cmd.ProcessState nil. Only log peak
248: 	// RSS if the compilation succeeded, which will be the majority of cases.
249: 	if usage, ok := compileCmd.ProcessState.SysUsage().(*syscall.Rusage); ok {
250: 		log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
251: 			"compiler-pid": compileCmd.Process.Pid,
252: 			"output":       output.Name(),
253: 		}).Debugf("Compilation had peak RSS of %d bytes", usage.Maxrss)
254: 	}
255: 
256: 	return output.Name(), nil
257: }
258: 
259: // compileDatapath invokes the compiler and linker to create all state files for
260: // the BPF datapath, with the primary target being the BPF ELF binary.
261: //
262: // It also creates the following output files:
263: // * Preprocessed C
264: // * Assembly
265: // * Object compiled with debug symbols
266: func compileDatapath(ctx context.Context, dirs *directoryInfo, isHost bool, logger *logrus.Entry) error {
267: 	scopedLog := logger.WithField(logfields.Debug, true)
268: 
269: 	versionCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, compiler, "--version")
270: 	compilerVersion, err := versionCmd.CombinedOutput(scopedLog, true)
271: 	if err != nil {
272: 		return err
273: 	}
274: 	scopedLog.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
275: 		compiler: string(compilerVersion),
276: 	}).Debug("Compiling datapath")
277: 
278: 	prog := epProg
279: 	if isHost {
280: 		prog = hostEpProg
281: 	}
282: 
283: 	if option.Config.Debug && prog.OutputType == outputObject {
284: 		// Write out preprocessing files for debugging purposes
285: 		debugProg := *prog
286: 		debugProg.Output = debugProg.Source
287: 		debugProg.OutputType = outputSource
288: 
289: 		if _, err := compile(ctx, &debugProg, dirs); err != nil {
290: 			// Only log an error here if the context was not canceled. This log message
291: 			// should only represent failures with respect to compiling the program.
292: 			if !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
293: 				scopedLog.WithField(logfields.Params, logfields.Repr(debugProg)).WithError(err).Debug("JoinEP: Failed to compile")
294: 			}
295: 			return err
296: 		}
297: 	}
298: 
299: 	if _, err := compile(ctx, prog, dirs); err != nil {
300: 		// Only log an error here if the context was not canceled. This log message
301: 		// should only represent failures with respect to compiling the program.
302: 		if !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
303: 			scopedLog.WithField(logfields.Params, logfields.Repr(prog)).WithError(err).Warn("JoinEP: Failed to compile")
304: 		}
305: 		return err
306: 	}
307: 
308: 	return nil
309: }
310: 
311: // compileWithOptions compiles a BPF program generating an object file,
312: // using a set of provided compiler options.
313: func compileWithOptions(ctx context.Context, src string, out string, opts []string) error {

324: 		State:   option.Config.StateDir,
325: 	}
326: 	_, err := compile(ctx, &prog, &dirs)
327: 	return err
328: }
329: 
330: // compileDefault compiles a BPF program generating an object file with default options.

24: )

26: // objectCache amortises the cost of BPF compilation for endpoints.
27: type objectCache struct {
28: 	lock.Mutex
29: 	datapath.ConfigWriter
30: 
31: 	// The directory used for caching. Must not be accessed by another process.
32: 	workingDirectory string
33: 
34: 	baseHash datapathHash
35: 
36: 	// The cached objects.
37: 	objects map[string]*cachedSpec
38: }
39: 
40: type cachedSpec struct {
41: 	// Protects state, also used to serialize compilation attempts.
42: 	lock.Mutex
43: 
44: 	// The compiled and parsed spec. May be nil if no compilation has happened yet.
45: 	spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec
46: }
47: 
48: func newObjectCache(c datapath.ConfigWriter, workingDir string) *objectCache {
49: 	return &objectCache{
50: 		ConfigWriter:     c,
51: 		workingDirectory: workingDir,
52: 		objects:          make(map[string]*cachedSpec),
53: 	}
54: }
55: 
56: // UpdateDatapathHash invalidates the object cache if the configuration of the
57: // datapath has changed.
58: func (o *objectCache) UpdateDatapathHash(nodeCfg *datapath.LocalNodeConfiguration) error {
59: 	newHash, err := hashDatapath(o.ConfigWriter, nodeCfg)
60: 	if err != nil {
61: 		return fmt.Errorf("hash datapath config: %w", err)
62: 	}
63: 
64: 	// Prevent new compilation from starting.
65: 	o.Lock()
66: 	defer o.Unlock()
67: 
68: 	// Don't invalidate if the hash is the same.
69: 	if bytes.Equal(newHash, o.baseHash) {
70: 		return nil
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	// Wait until all concurrent compilation has finished.
74: 	for _, obj := range o.objects {
75: 		obj.Lock()
76: 	}
77: 
78: 	if err := os.RemoveAll(o.workingDirectory); err != nil {
79: 		for _, obj := range o.objects {
80: 			obj.Unlock()
81: 		}
82: 
83: 		return err
84: 	}
85: 	// Unlock all objects so that race detector doesn't complain about potential
86: 	// deadlocks.
87: 	for _, obj := range o.objects {
88: 		obj.Unlock()
89: 	}
90: 
91: 	o.baseHash = newHash
92: 	o.objects = make(map[string]*cachedSpec)
93: 	return nil
94: }
95: 
96: // serialize access to an abitrary key.
97: //
98: // The caller must call Unlock on the returned object.
99: func (o *objectCache) serialize(key string) *cachedSpec {
100: 	o.Lock()
101: 	defer o.Unlock()
102: 
103: 	obj, ok := o.objects[key]
104: 	if !ok {
105: 		obj = new(cachedSpec)
106: 		o.objects[key] = obj
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	obj.Lock()
110: 	return obj
111: }
112: 
113: // build attempts to compile and cache a datapath template object file
114: // corresponding to the specified endpoint configuration.
115: func (o *objectCache) build(ctx context.Context, nodeCfg *datapath.LocalNodeConfiguration, cfg datapath.EndpointConfiguration, stats *metrics.SpanStat, dir *directoryInfo, hash string) (string, error) {
116: 	isHost := cfg.IsHost()
117: 	templatePath := filepath.Join(o.workingDirectory, hash)
118: 	dir = &directoryInfo{
119: 		Library: dir.Library,
120: 		Runtime: dir.Runtime,
121: 		Output:  templatePath,
122: 		State:   templatePath,
123: 	}
124: 	prog := epProg
125: 	if isHost {
126: 		prog = hostEpProg
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	objectPath := prog.AbsoluteOutput(dir)
130: 
131: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir.Output, defaults.StateDirRights); err != nil {
132: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create template directory: %w", err)
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	headerPath := filepath.Join(dir.State, common.CHeaderFileName)
136: 	f, err := os.Create(headerPath)
137: 	if err != nil {
138: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to open template header for writing: %w", err)
139: 	}
140: 	defer f.Close()
141: 	if err = o.ConfigWriter.WriteEndpointConfig(f, nodeCfg, cfg); err != nil {
142: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to write template header: %w", err)
143: 	}
144: 
145: 	stats.BpfCompilation.Start()
146: 	err = compileDatapath(ctx, dir, isHost, log)
147: 	stats.BpfCompilation.End(err == nil)
148: 	if err != nil {
149: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to compile template program: %w", err)
150: 	}
151: 
152: 	log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
153: 		logfields.Path:               objectPath,
154: 		logfields.BPFCompilationTime: stats.BpfCompilation.Total(),
155: 	}).Info("Compiled new BPF template")
156: 
157: 	return objectPath, nil
158: }
159: 
160: // fetchOrCompile attempts to fetch the path to the datapath object
161: // corresponding to the provided endpoint configuration, or if this
162: // configuration is not yet compiled, compiles it. It will block if multiple
163: // threads attempt to concurrently fetchOrCompile a template binary for the
164: // same set of EndpointConfiguration.
165: //
166: // Returns a copy of the compiled and parsed ELF and a hash identifying a cached entry.
167: func (o *objectCache) fetchOrCompile(ctx context.Context, nodeCfg *datapath.LocalNodeConfiguration, cfg datapath.EndpointConfiguration, dir *directoryInfo, stats *metrics.SpanStat) (spec *ebpf.CollectionSpec, hash string, err error) {
168: 	cfg = wrap(cfg)
169: 
170: 	hash, err = o.baseHash.hashTemplate(o, nodeCfg, cfg)
171: 	if err != nil {
172: 		return nil, "", err
173: 	}
174: 
175: 	// Capture the time spent waiting for the template to compile.
176: 	if stats != nil {
177: 		stats.BpfWaitForELF.Start()
178: 		defer func() {
179: 			// Wrap to ensure that "err" is compared upon return.
180: 			stats.BpfWaitForELF.End(err == nil)
181: 		}()
182: 	}
183: 
184: 	scopedLog := log.WithField(logfields.BPFHeaderfileHash, hash)
185: 
186: 	// Only allow a single concurrent compilation per hash.
187: 	obj := o.serialize(hash)
188: 	defer obj.Unlock()
189: 
190: 	// The serialize call might have blocked for a significant amount of time
191: 	// if another compilation was in progress. Make sure that the endpoint is
192: 	// still alive, to bail out early otherwise, and prevent doing unnecessary
193: 	// operations that would likely fail.
194: 	select {
195: 	case <-ctx.Done():
196: 		return nil, "", ctx.Err()
197: 	default:
198: 	}
199: 
200: 	if obj.spec != nil {
201: 		return obj.spec.Copy(), hash, nil
202: 	}
203: 
204: 	if stats == nil {
205: 		stats = &metrics.SpanStat{}
206: 	}
207: 
208: 	path, err := o.build(ctx, nodeCfg, cfg, stats, dir, hash)
209: 	if err != nil {
210: 		if !errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
211: 			scopedLog.WithError(err).Error("BPF template object creation failed")
212: 		}
213: 		return nil, "", err
214: 	}
215: 
216: 	obj.spec, err = bpf.LoadCollectionSpec(path)
217: 	if err != nil {
218: 		return nil, "", fmt.Errorf("load eBPF ELF %s: %w", path, err)
219: 	}
220: 
221: 	return obj.spec.Copy(), hash, nil
222: }

err != nil {\n826: \t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"unable to write header file: %w\", err)\n827: \t\t}\n828: \n829: \t\tdatapathRegenCtxt.epInfoCache = e.createEpInfoCache(nextDir)\n830: \t} else {\n831: \t\tdatapathRegenCtxt.epInfoCache = e.createEpInfoCache(currentDir)\n832: \t}\n833: \n834: \treturn nil\n835: }\n836: \n837: func (e *Endpoint) finalizeProxyState(regenContext *regenerationContext, err error) {\n838: \tdatapathRegenCtx := regenContext.datapathRegenerationContext\n839: \tif err == nil {\n840: \t\t// Always execute the finalization code, even if the endpoint is\n841: \t\t// terminating, in order to properly release resources.\n842: \t\te.unconditionalLock()\n843: \t\tdefer e.unlock() // In case Finalize() panics\n844: \t\te.getLogger().Debug(\"Finalizing successful endpoint regeneration\")\n845: \t\tdatapathRegenCtx.finalizeList.Finalize()\n846: \t} else {\n847: \t\tif err := e.lockAlive(); 17: 
18: var (

23: 		cell.Provide(newRegenerator),

25: )
26: 
27: // KVStoreNodesWaitFn is the type of the function used to wait for synchronization
28: // of all nodes from the kvstore.
29: type KVStoreNodesWaitFn wait.Fn
30: 
31: // Regenerator wraps additional functionalities for endpoint regeneration.
32: type Regenerator struct {
33: 	nodesWaitFn   KVStoreNodesWaitFn
34: 	cmWaitFn      wait.Fn
35: 	cmWaitTimeout time.Duration
36: 
37: 	logger        logrus.FieldLogger
38: 	cmSyncLogOnce sync.Once
39: }
40: 
41: func newRegenerator(in struct {
42: 	cell.In
43: 
44: 	Logger logrus.FieldLogger
45: 
46: 	Config      wait.TimeoutConfig
47: 	NodesWaitFn KVStoreNodesWaitFn
48: 	ClusterMesh *clustermesh.ClusterMesh
49: }) *Regenerator {
50: 	waitFn := func(context.Context) error { return nil }
51: 	if in.ClusterMesh != nil {
52: 		waitFn = in.ClusterMesh.IPIdentitiesSynced
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	return &Regenerator{
56: 		logger:        in.Logger,
57: 		nodesWaitFn:   in.NodesWaitFn,
58: 		cmWaitFn:      waitFn,
59: 		cmWaitTimeout: in.Config.ClusterMeshSyncTimeout,
60: 	}
61: }
62: 
63: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForKVStoreNodesSync(ctx context.Context) error {
64: 	return r.nodesWaitFn(ctx)
65: }
66: 
67: func (r *Regenerator) WaitForClusterMeshIPIdentitiesSync(ctx context.Context) error {
68: 	wctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, r.cmWaitTimeout)
69: 	defer cancel()
70: 	err := r.cmWaitFn(wctx)
71: 
72: 	switch {
73: 	case ctx.Err() != nil:
74: 		// The context associated with the endpoint has been canceled.
75: 		return ErrNotAlive
76: 	case err != nil:
77: 		// We don't return an error in case the wait operation timed out, as we can
78: 		// continue with the endpoint regeneration, although at the cost of possible
79: 		// connectivity drops for cross-cluster connections. We additionally print
80: 		// the warning message only once, to avoid repeating it for every endpoint.
81: 		r.cmSyncLogOnce.Do(func() {
82: 			r.logger.Warning("Failed waiting for clustermesh IPs and identities synchronization before regenerating endpoints, expect possible disruption of cross-cluster connections")
83: 		})
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	return nil
87: }

see\n99: \t// iptables.addCiliumENIRules().\n100: \t// The default AWS daemonset installs the following rules that are used\n101: \t// for NodePort traffic between nodes:\n102: \t//\n103: \t// # sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.eth0.rp_filter=2\n104: \t// # iptables -t mangle -A PREROUTING -i eth0 -m comment --comment \"AWS, primary ENI\" -m addrtype --dst-type LOCAL --limit-iface-in -j CONNMARK --set-xmark 0x80/0x80\n105: \t// # iptables -t mangle -A PREROUTING -i eni+ -m comment --comment \"AWS, primary ENI\" -j CONNMARK --restore-mark --nfmask 0x80 --ctmask 0x80\n106: \t// # ip rule add fwmark 0x80/0x80 lookup main\n107: \t//\n108: \t// It marks packets coming from another node through eth0, and restores\n109: \t// the mark on the return path to force a lookup into the main routing\n110: \t// table. Without these rules, the \"ip rules\" set by the cilium-cni\n111: \t// plugin tell the host to lookup into the table related to the VPC for\n112: \t// which the CIDR used by the endpoint has been configured.\n113: \t//\n114: \t// We want to reproduce equivalent rules to ensure correct routing.\n115: \tif !option.Config.EnableIPv4 {\n116: \t\treturn sysSettings, nil\n117: \t}\n118: \n119: \tiface, err := route.NodeDeviceWithDefaultRoute(option.Config.EnableIPv4, option.Config.EnableIPv6)\n120: \tif err != nil {\n121: \t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to find interface with default route: %w\", err)\n122: \t}\n123: \n124: \tretSettings := append(sysSettings, tables.Sysctl{\n125: \t\tName:      []string{\"net\", \"ipv4\", \"conf\", iface.Attrs().Name, \"rp_filter\"},\n126: \t\tVal:       \"2\",\n127: \t\tIgnoreErr: false,\n128: \t})\n129: \tif err := route.ReplaceRule(route.Rule{\n130: \t\tPriority: linux_defaults.RulePriorityNodeport,\n131: \t\tMark:     linux_defaults.MarkMultinodeNodeport,\n132: \t\tMask:     linux_defaults.MaskMultinodeNodeport,\n133: \t\tTable:    route.MainTable,\n134: \t\tProtocol: linux_defaults.RTProto,\n135: \t}); 15: // bpffsDevicesDir returns the path to the 'devices' directory on bpffs, usually
16: // /sys/fs/bpf/cilium/devices. It does not ensure the directory exists.
17: //
18: // base is typically set to /sys/fs/bpf/cilium, but can be a temp directory
19: // during tests.
20: func bpffsDevicesDir(base string) string {
21: 	return filepath.Join(base, "devices")
22: }
23: 
24: // bpffsDeviceDir returns the path to the per-device directory on bpffs, usually
25: // /sys/fs/bpf/cilium/devices/<device>. It does not ensure the directory exists.
26: //
27: // base is typically set to /sys/fs/bpf/cilium, but can be a temp directory
28: // during tests.
29: func bpffsDeviceDir(base string, device netlink.Link) string {
30: 	// If a device name contains a "." we must sanitize the string to satisfy bpffs directory path
31: 	// requirements. The string of a directory path on bpffs is not allowed to contain any "." characters.
32: 	// By replacing "." with "-", we circurmvent this limitation. This also introduces a small
33: 	// risk of naming collisions, e.g "eth-0" and "eth.0" would translate to the same bpffs directory.
34: 	// The probability of this happening in practice should be very small.
35: 	return filepath.Join(bpffsDevicesDir(base), strings.ReplaceAll(device.Attrs().Name, ".", "-"))
36: }
37: 
38: // bpffsDeviceLinksDir returns the bpffs path to the per-device links directory,
39: // usually /sys/fs/bpf/cilium/devices/<device>/links. It does not ensure the
40: // directory exists.
41: //
42: // base is typically set to /sys/fs/bpf/cilium, but can be a temp directory
43: // during tests.
44: func bpffsDeviceLinksDir(base string, device netlink.Link) string {
45: 	return filepath.Join(bpffsDeviceDir(base, device), "links")
46: }
47: 
48: // bpffsEndpointsDir returns the path to the 'endpoints' directory on bpffs, usually
49: // /sys/fs/bpf/cilium/endpoints. It does not ensure the directory exists.
50: //
51: // base is typically set to /sys/fs/bpf/cilium, but can be a temp directory
52: // during tests.
53: func bpffsEndpointsDir(base string) string {
54: 	return filepath.Join(base, "endpoints")
55: }
56: 
57: // bpffsEndpointDir returns the path to the per-endpoint directory on bpffs,
58: // usually /sys/fs/bpf/cilium/endpoints/<endpoint-id>. It does not ensure the
59: // directory exists.
60: //
61: // base is typically set to /sys/fs/bpf/cilium, but can be a temp directory
62: // during tests.
63: func bpffsEndpointDir(base string, ep datapath.Endpoint) string {
64: 	return filepath.Join(bpffsEndpointsDir(base), ep.StringID())
65: }
66: 
67: // bpffsEndpointLinksDir returns the bpffs path to the per-endpoint links directory,
68: // usually /sys/fs/bpf/cilium/endpoints/<endpoint-id>/links. It does not ensure the
69: // directory exists.
70: //
71: // base is typically set to /sys/fs/bpf/cilium, but can be a temp directory
72: // during tests.
73: func bpffsEndpointLinksDir(base string, ep datapath.Endpoint) string {
74: 	return filepath.Join(bpffsEndpointDir(base, ep), "links")
75: }

128: 		log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{
129: 			logfields.PolicyRevision: rev,
130: 			logfields.EndpointID:     e.ID,
131: 		}).Errorf("enqueue of EndpointRevisionBumpEvent failed: %s", err)
132: 	}
133: }

135: // EndpointNoTrackEvent contains all fields necessary to update the NOTRACK rules.
136: type EndpointNoTrackEvent struct {
137: 	ep      *Endpoint
138: 	portStr string
139: }
140: 
141: // Handle handles the NOTRACK rule update.
142: func (ev *EndpointNoTrackEvent) Handle(res chan interface{}) {
143: 	var port uint16
144: 
145: 	e := ev.ep
146: 
147: 	// If this endpoint is going away, nothing to do.
148: 	if err := e.lockAlive(); err != nil {
149: 		res <- &EndpointRegenerationResult{
150: 			err: nil,
151: 		}
152: 		return
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	defer e.unlock()
156: 
157: 	if ev.portStr == "" {
158: 		port = 0
159: 	} else {
160: 		// Validate annotation before we do any actual alteration to the endpoint.
161: 		p64, err := strconv.ParseUint(ev.portStr, 10, 16)
162: 		// Port should be within [1-65535].
163: 		if err != nil || p64 == 0 {
164: 			res <- &EndpointRegenerationResult{
165: 				err: err,
166: 			}
167: 			return
168: 		}
169: 		port = uint16(p64)
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	if port != e.noTrackPort {
173: 		log.Debug("Updating NOTRACK rules")
174: 		if option.Config.EnableIPv4 && e.IPv4.IsValid() {
175: 			if port > 0 {
176: 				e.owner.IPTablesManager().InstallNoTrackRules(e.IPv4, port)
177: 			}
178: 			if e.noTrackPort > 0 {
179: 				e.owner.IPTablesManager().RemoveNoTrackRules(e.IPv4, e.noTrackPort)
180: 			}
181: 		}
182: 		if option.Config.EnableIPv6 && e.IPv6.IsValid() {
183: 			if port > 0 {
184: 				e.owner.IPTablesManager().InstallNoTrackRules(e.IPv6, port)
185: 			}
186: 			if e.noTrackPort > 0 {
187: 				e.owner.IPTablesManager().RemoveNoTrackRules(e.IPv6, e.noTrackPort)
188: 			}
189: 		}
190: 		e.noTrackPort = port
191: 	}
192: 
193: 	res <- &EndpointRegenerationResult{
194: 		err: nil,
195: 	}
196: }
197: 
198: // EndpointPolicyBandwidthEvent contains all fields necessary to update
199: // the Pod's bandwidth policy.
200: type EndpointPolicyBandwidthEvent struct {
201: 	bwm             datapath.BandwidthManager
202: 	ep              *Endpoint
203: 	bandwidthEgress string
204: 	priority        string
205: }
206: 
207: // Handle handles the policy bandwidth update.
208: func (ev *EndpointPolicyBandwidthEvent) Handle(res chan interface{}) {
209: 	var bps, prio uint64
210: 
211: 	if !ev.bwm.Enabled() {
212: 		res <- &EndpointRegenerationResult{
213: 			err: nil,
214: 		}
215: 		return
216: 	}
217: 
218: 	e := ev.ep
219: 	if err := e.lockAlive(); err != nil {
220: 		// If the endpoint is being deleted, we don't need to
221: 		// update its bandwidth policy.
222: 		res <- &EndpointRegenerationResult{
223: 			err: nil,
224: 		}
225: 		return
226: 	}
227: 	defer func() {
228: 		e.unlock()
229: 	}()
230: 
231: 	bwmUpdateNeeded := false
232: 	var err error
233: 	if ev.bandwidthEgress != "" {
234: 		bps, err = bandwidth.GetBytesPerSec(ev.bandwidthEgress)
235: 	}
236: 	if err != nil {
237: 		e.getLogger().WithError(err).Debugf("failed to parse bandwidth limit %q", ev.bandwidthEgress)
238: 	} else {
239: 		bwmUpdateNeeded = true
240: 	}
241: 	if ev.priority != "" {
242: 		ev.priority = strings.ReplaceAll(ev.priority, "-", "")
243: 		switch strings.ToLower(ev.priority) {
244: 		case "besteffort":
245: 			prio = bandwidth.BestEffortQoSDefaultPriority
246: 		case "burstable":
247: 			prio = bandwidth.BurstableQoSDefaultPriority
248: 		case "guaranteed":
249: 			prio = bandwidth.GuaranteedQoSDefaultPriority
250: 		default:
251: 			// Also support explicitly setting priority values.
252: 			prio, err = strconv.ParseUint(ev.priority, 10, 32)
253: 			if err != nil {
254: 				e.getLogger().WithError(err).Debugf("failed to parse priority value %q", ev.priority)
255: 			} else {
256: 				prio += 1
257: 			}
258: 		}
259: 	}
260: 	if err != nil {
261: 		e.getLogger().WithError(err).Debugf("failed to parse priority value limit %q", ev.priority)
262: 	} else {
263: 		bwmUpdateNeeded = true
264: 	}
265: 
266: 	if bwmUpdateNeeded {
267: 		ev.bwm.UpdateBandwidthLimit(e.ID, bps, uint32(prio))
268: 	} else {
269: 		ev.bwm.DeleteBandwidthLimit(e.ID)
270: 	}
271: 	if err != nil {
272: 		res <- &EndpointRegenerationResult{
273: 			err: err,
274: 		}
275: 		return
276: 	}
277: 
278: 	bpsOld := "inf"
279: 	bpsNew := "inf"
280: 	if e.bps != 0 {
281: 		bpsOld = strconv.FormatUint(e.bps, 10)
282: 	}
283: 	if bps != 0 {
284: 		bpsNew = strconv.FormatUint(bps, 10)
285: 	}
286: 	e.getLogger().Debugf("Updating %s from %s to %s bytes/sec", bandwidth.EgressBandwidth,
287: 		bpsOld, bpsNew)
288: 	e.bps = bps
289: 	res <- &EndpointRegenerationResult{
290: 		err: nil,
291: 	}
292: }
293: 
294: // InitEventQueue initializes the endpoint's event queue. Note that this
295: // function does not begin processing events off the queue, as that's left up
296: // to the caller to call Expose in order to allow other subsystems to access
297: // the endpoint. This function assumes that the endpoint ID has already been
298: // allocated!
299: //
300: // Having this be a separate function allows us to prepare
301: // the event queue while the endpoint is being validated (during restoration)
302: // so that when its metadata is resolved, events can be enqueued (such as
303: // bandwidth policy).
304: func (e *Endpoint) InitEventQueue() {
305: 	e.eventQueue = eventqueue.NewEventQueueBuffered(fmt.Sprintf("endpoint-%d", e.ID), option.Config.EndpointQueueSize)
306: }
307: 
308: // Start assigns a Cilium Endpoint ID to the endpoint and prepares it to
309: // receive events from other subsystems.
310: //
311: // The endpoint must not already be exposed via the endpointmanager prior to
312: // calling Start(), as it assumes unconditional access over the Endpoint
313: // object.
314: func (e *Endpoint) Start(id uint16) {
315: 	// No need to check liveness as an endpoint can only be deleted via the
316: 	// API after it has been inserted into the manager.
317: 	// 'e.ID' written below, read lock is not enough.
318: 	e.unconditionalLock()
319: 	defer e.unlock()
320: 
321: 	e.ID = id
322: 	e.UpdateLogger(map[string]interface{}{
323: 		logfields.EndpointID: e.ID,
324: 	})
325: 
326: 	// Start goroutines that are responsible for handling events.
327: 	e.startRegenerationFailureHandler()
328: 	if e.eventQueue == nil {
329: 		e.InitEventQueue()
330: 	}
331: 	e.eventQueue.Run()
332: 	e.getLogger().Info("New endpoint")
333: }
334: 
335: // Stop cleans up all goroutines managed by this endpoint (EventQueue,
336: // Controllers).
337: // This function should be used directly in cleanup functions which aim to stop
338: // goroutines managed by this endpoint, but without removing BPF maps and
339: // datapath state (for instance, because the daemon is shutting down but the
340: // endpoint should remain operational while the daemon is not running).
341: func (e *Endpoint) Stop() {
342: 	// Since the endpoint is being deleted, we no longer need to run events
343: 	// in its event queue. This is a no-op if the queue has already been
344: 	// closed elsewhere.
345: 	e.eventQueue.Stop()
346: 
347: 	// Cancel active controllers for the endpoint tied to e.aliveCtx.
348: 	// Needs to be performed before draining the event queue to allow
349: 	// in-flight functions to act before the Endpoint's underlying resources
350: 	// are removed by the container runtime.
351: 	e.aliveCancel()
352: 
353: 	// Wait for the queue to be drained in case an event which is currently
354: 	// running for the endpoint tries to acquire the lock - we cannot be sure
355: 	// what types of events will be pushed onto the EventQueue for an endpoint
356: 	// and when they will happen. After this point, no events for the endpoint
357: 	// will be processed on its EventQueue, specifically regenerations.
358: 	e.eventQueue.WaitToBeDrained()
359: 
360: 	// Given that we are deleting the endpoint and that no more builds are
361: 	// going to occur for this endpoint, close the channel which signals whether
362: 	// the endpoint has its BPF program compiled or not to avoid it persisting
363: 	// if anything is blocking on it. If a delete request has already been
364: 	// enqueued for this endpoint, this is a no-op.
365: 	e.closeBPFProgramChannel()
366: }

"structuredContent": {"content": "1: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2: // Copyright Authors of Cilium

4: package policy
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"errors"
8: 	"fmt"
9: 	"iter"
10: 	"runtime"

12: 	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"

20: // SelectorPolicy represents a selectorPolicy, previously resolved from
21: // the policy repository and ready to be distilled against a set of identities
22: // to compute datapath-level policy configuration.
23: type SelectorPolicy interface {
24: 	// CreateRedirects is used to ensure the endpoint has created all the needed redirects
25: 	// before a new EndpointPolicy is created.
26: 	RedirectFilters() iter.Seq2[*L4Filter, *PerSelectorPolicy]
27: 
28: 	// DistillPolicy returns the policy in terms of connectivity to peer
29: 	// Identities.
30: 	DistillPolicy(owner PolicyOwner, redirects map[string]uint16) *EndpointPolicy
31: }
32: 
33: // selectorPolicy is a structure which contains the resolved policy for a
34: // particular Identity across all layers (L3, L4, and L7), with the policy
35: // still determined in terms of EndpointSelectors.
36: type selectorPolicy struct {
37: 	// Revision is the revision of the policy repository used to generate
38: 	// this selectorPolicy.
39: 	Revision uint64
40: 
41: 	// SelectorCache managing selectors in L4Policy
42: 	SelectorCache *SelectorCache
43: 
44: 	// L4Policy contains the computed L4 and L7 policy.
45: 	L4Policy L4Policy
46: 
47: 	// IngressPolicyEnabled specifies whether this policy contains any policy
48: 	// at ingress.
49: 	IngressPolicyEnabled bool
50: 
51: 	// EgressPolicyEnabled specifies whether this policy contains any policy
52: 	// at egress.
53: 	EgressPolicyEnabled bool
54: }
55: 
56: func (p *selectorPolicy) Attach(ctx PolicyContext) {
57: 	p.L4Policy.Attach(ctx)
58: }
59: 
60: // EndpointPolicy is a structure which contains the resolved policy across all
61: // layers (L3, L4, and L7), distilled against a set of identities.
62: type EndpointPolicy struct {
63: 	// Note that all Endpoints sharing the same identity will be
64: 	// referring to a shared selectorPolicy!
65: 	*selectorPolicy
66: 
67: 	// VersionHandle represents the version of the SelectorCache 'policyMapState' was generated
68: 	// from.
69: 	// Changes after this version appear in 'policyMapChanges'.
70: 	// This is updated when incremental changes are applied.
71: 	VersionHandle *versioned.VersionHandle
72: 
73: 	// policyMapState contains the state of this policy as it relates to the
74: 	// datapath. In the future, this will be factored out of this object to
75: 	// decouple the policy as it relates to the datapath vs. its userspace
76: 	// representation.
77: 	// It maps each Key to the proxy port if proxy redirection is needed.
78: 	// Proxy port 0 indicates no proxy redirection.
79: 	// All fields within the Key and the proxy port must be in host byte-order.
80: 	// Must only be accessed with PolicyOwner (aka Endpoint) lock taken.
81: 	policyMapState mapState
82: 
83: 	// policyMapChanges collects pending changes to the PolicyMapState
84: 	policyMapChanges MapChanges
85: 
86: 	// PolicyOwner describes any type which consumes this EndpointPolicy object.
87: 	PolicyOwner PolicyOwner
88: 
89: 	// Redirects contains the proxy ports needed for this EndpointPolicy.
90: 	// If any redirects are missing a new policy will be computed to rectify it, so this is
91: 	// constant for the lifetime of this EndpointPolicy.
92: 	Redirects map[string]uint16
93: }
94: 
95: // LookupRedirectPort returns the redirect L4 proxy port for the given input parameters.
96: // Returns 0 if not found or the filter doesn't require a redirect.
97: // Returns an error if the redirect port can not be found.
98: // This is called when accumulating incremental map changes, endpoint lock must not be taken.
99: func (p *EndpointPolicy) LookupRedirectPort(ingress bool, protocol string, port uint16, listener string) (uint16, error) {
100: 	proxyID := ProxyID(uint16(p.PolicyOwner.GetID()), ingress, protocol, port, listener)
101: 	if proxyPort, exists := p.Redirects[proxyID]; exists {
102: 		return proxyPort, nil
103: 	}
104: 	return 0, fmt.Errorf("Proxy port for redirect %q not found", proxyID)
105: }
106: 
107: // CopyMapStateFrom copies the policy map entries from m.
108: func (p *EndpointPolicy) CopyMapStateFrom(m MapStateMap) {
109: 	for key, entry := range m {
110: 		p.policyMapState.entries[key] = mapStateEntry{
111: 			MapStateEntry: entry,
112: 		}
113: 	}
114: }
115: 
116: // PolicyOwner is anything which consumes a EndpointPolicy.
117: type PolicyOwner interface {
118: 	GetID() uint64
119: 	GetNamedPort(ingress bool, name string, proto u8proto.U8proto) uint16
120: 	PolicyDebug(fields logrus.Fields, msg string)
121: 	IsHost() bool
122: 	MapStateSize() int
123: }
124: 
125: // newSelectorPolicy returns an empty selectorPolicy stub.
126: func newSelectorPolicy(selectorCache *SelectorCache) *selectorPolicy {
127: 	return &selectorPolicy{
128: 		Revision:      0,
129: 		SelectorCache: selectorCache,
130: 		L4Policy:      NewL4Policy(0),
131: 	}
132: }
133: 
134: // insertUser adds a user to the L4Policy so that incremental
135: // updates of the L4Policy may be fowarded.
136: func (p *selectorPolicy) insertUser(user *EndpointPolicy) {
137: 	p.L4Policy.insertUser(user)
138: }
139: 
140: // removeUser removes a user from the L4Policy so the EndpointPolicy
141: // can be freed when not needed any more
142: func (p *selectorPolicy) removeUser(user *EndpointPolicy) {
143: 	p.L4Policy.removeUser(user)
144: }
145: 
146: // Detach releases resources held by a selectorPolicy to enable
147: // successful eventual GC.  Note that the selectorPolicy itself if not
148: // modified in any way, so that it can be used concurrently.
149: func (p *selectorPolicy) Detach() {
150: 	p.L4Policy.Detach(p.SelectorCache)
151: }
152: 
153: // DistillPolicy filters down the specified selectorPolicy (which acts
154: // upon selectors) into a set of concrete map entries based on the
155: // SelectorCache. These can subsequently be plumbed into the datapath.
156: //
157: // Called without holding the Selector cache or Repository locks.
158: // PolicyOwner (aka Endpoint) is also unlocked during this call,
159: // but the Endpoint's build mutex is held.
160: func (p *selectorPolicy) DistillPolicy(policyOwner PolicyOwner, redirects map[string]uint16) *EndpointPolicy {
161: 	var calculatedPolicy *EndpointPolicy
162: 
163: 	// EndpointPolicy is initialized while 'GetCurrentVersionHandleFunc' keeps the selector
164: 	// cache write locked. This syncronizes the SelectorCache handle creation and the insertion
165: 	// of the new policy to the selectorPolicy before any new incremental updated can be
166: 	// generated.
167: 	//
168: 	// With this we have to following guarantees:
169: 	// - Selections seen with the 'version' are the ones available at the time of the 'version'
170: 	//   creation, and the IDs therein have been applied to all Selectors cached at the time.
171: 	// - All further incremental updates are delivered to 'policyMapChanges' as whole
172: 	//   transactions, i.e, changes to all selectors due to addition or deletion of new/old
173: 	//   identities are visible in the set of changes processed and returned by
174: 	//   ConsumeMapChanges().
175: 	p.SelectorCache.GetVersionHandleFunc(func(version *versioned.VersionHandle) {
176: 		calculatedPolicy = &EndpointPolicy{
177: 			selectorPolicy: p,
178: 			VersionHandle:  version,
179: 			policyMapState: newMapState(policyOwner.MapStateSize()),
180: 			policyMapChanges: MapChanges{
181: 				firstVersion: version.Version(),
182: 			},
183: 			PolicyOwner: policyOwner,
184: 			Redirects:   redirects,
185: 		}
186: 		// Register the new EndpointPolicy as a receiver of incremental
187: 		// updates before selector cache lock is released by 'GetCurrentVersionHandleFunc'.
188: 		p.insertUser(calculatedPolicy)
189: 	})
190: 
191: 	if !p.IngressPolicyEnabled || !p.EgressPolicyEnabled {
192: 		calculatedPolicy.policyMapState.allowAllIdentities(
193: 			!p.IngressPolicyEnabled, !p.EgressPolicyEnabled)
194: 	}
195: 
196: 	// Must come after the 'insertUser()' above to guarantee
197: 	// PolicyMapChanges will contain all changes that are applied
198: 	// after the computation of PolicyMapState has started.
199: 	calculatedPolicy.toMapState()
200: 	if !policyOwner.IsHost() {
201: 		calculatedPolicy.policyMapState.determineAllowLocalhostIngress()
202: 	}
203: 
204: 	return calculatedPolicy
205: }
206: 
207: // Ready releases the handle on a selector cache version so that stale state can be released.
208: // This should be called when the policy has been realized.
209: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Ready() (err error) {
210: 	// release resources held for this version
211: 	err = p.VersionHandle.Close()
212: 	p.VersionHandle = nil
213: 	return err
214: }
215: 
216: // Detach removes EndpointPolicy references from selectorPolicy
217: // to allow the EndpointPolicy to be GC'd.
218: // PolicyOwner (aka Endpoint) is also locked during this call.
219: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Detach() {
220: 	p.selectorPolicy.removeUser(p)
221: 	// in case the call was missed previouly
222: 	if p.Ready() == nil {
223: 		// succeeded, so it was missed previously
224: 		_, file, line, _ := runtime.Caller(1)
225: 		log.Warningf("Detach: EndpointPolicy was not marked as Ready (%s:%d)", file, line)
226: 	}
227: 	// Also release the version handle held for incremental updates, if any.
228: 	// This must be done after the removeUser() call above, so that we do not get a new version
229: 	// handles any more!
230: 	p.policyMapChanges.detach()
231: }

233: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Len() int {
234: 	return p.policyMapState.Len()
235: }
236: 
237: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Get(key Key) (MapStateEntry, bool) {
238: 	return p.policyMapState.Get(key)
239: }
240: 
241: var errMissingKey = errors.New("Key not found")
242: 
243: // GetRuleLabels returns the list of labels of the rules that contributed
244: // to the entry at this key.
245: // The returned LabelArrayList is shallow-copied and therefore must not be mutated.
246: func (p *EndpointPolicy) GetRuleLabels(k Key) (labels.LabelArrayList, error) {
247: 	entry, ok := p.policyMapState.get(k)
248: 	if !ok {
249: 		return nil, errMissingKey
250: 	}
251: 	return entry.GetRuleLabels(), nil
252: }
253: 
254: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Entries() iter.Seq2[Key, MapStateEntry] {
255: 	return func(yield func(Key, MapStateEntry) bool) {
256: 		p.policyMapState.ForEach(yield)
257: 	}
258: }
259: 
260: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Equals(other MapStateMap) bool {
261: 	return p.policyMapState.Equals(other)
262: }
263: 
264: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Diff(expected MapStateMap) string {
265: 	return p.policyMapState.Diff(expected)
266: }
267: 
268: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Empty() bool {
269: 	return p.policyMapState.Empty()
270: }
271: 
272: // Updated returns an iterator for all key/entry pairs in 'p' that are either new or updated
273: // compared to the entries in 'realized'.
274: // Here 'realized' is another EndpointPolicy.
275: // This can be used to figure out which entries need to be added to or updated in 'realised'.
276: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Updated(realized *EndpointPolicy) iter.Seq2[Key, MapStateEntry] {
277: 	return func(yield func(Key, MapStateEntry) bool) {
278: 		p.policyMapState.ForEach(func(key Key, entry MapStateEntry) bool {
279: 			if oldEntry, ok := realized.policyMapState.Get(key); !ok || oldEntry != entry {
280: 				if !yield(key, entry) {
281: 					return false
282: 				}
283: 			}
284: 			return true
285: 		})
286: 	}
287: }
288: 
289: // Missing returns an iterator for all key/entry pairs in 'realized' that missing from 'p'.
290: // Here 'realized' is another EndpointPolicy.
291: // This can be used to figure out which entries in 'realised' need to be deleted.
292: func (p *EndpointPolicy) Missing(realized *EndpointPolicy) iter.Seq2[Key, MapStateEntry] {
293: 	return func(yield func(Key, MapStateEntry) bool) {
294: 		realized.policyMapState.ForEach(func(key Key, entry MapStateEntry) bool {
295: 			// If key that is in realized state is not in desired state, just remove it.
296: 			if _, ok := p.policyMapState.Get(key); !ok {
297: 				if !yield(key, entry) {
298: 					return false
299: 				}
300: 			}
301: 			return true
302: 		})
303: 	}
304: }
305: 
306: // UpdatedMap returns an iterator for all key/entry pairs in 'p' that are either new or updated
307: // compared to the entries in 'realized'.
308: // Here 'realized' is MapStateMap.
309: // This can be used to figure out which entries need to be added to or updated in 'realised'.
310: func (p *EndpointPolicy) UpdatedMap(realized MapStateMap) iter.Seq2[Key, MapStateEntry] {
311: 	return func(yield func(Key, MapStateEntry) bool) {
312: 		p.policyMapState.ForEach(func(key Key, entry MapStateEntry) bool {
313: 			if oldEntry, ok := realized[key]; !ok || oldEntry != entry {
314: 				if !yield(key, entry) {
315: 					return false
316: 				}
317: 			}
318: 			return true
319: 		})
320: 	}
321: }
322: 
323: // Missing returns an iterator for all key/entry pairs in 'realized' that missing from 'p'.
324: // Here 'realized' is MapStateMap.
325: // This can be used to figure out which entries in 'realised' need to be deleted.
326: func (p *EndpointPolicy) MissingMap(realized MapStateMap) iter.Seq2[Key, MapStateEntry] {
327: 	return func(yield func(Key, MapStateEntry) bool) {
328: 		for k, v := range realized {
329: 			// If key that is in realized state is not in desired state, just remove it.
330: 			if _, ok := p.policyMapState.Get(k); !ok {
331: 				if !yield(k, v) {
332: 					break
333: 				}
334: 			}
335: 		}
336: 	}
337: }
338: 
339: func (p *EndpointPolicy) RevertChanges(changes ChangeState) {
340: 	// SelectorCache used as Identities interface which only has GetPrefix() that needs no lock
341: 	p.policyMapState.revertChanges(changes)
342: }
343: 
344: // toMapState transforms the EndpointPolicy.L4Policy into
345: // the datapath-friendly format inside EndpointPolicy.PolicyMapState.
346: // Called with selectorcache locked for reading.
347: // Called without holding the Repository lock.
348: // PolicyOwner (aka Endpoint) is also unlocked during this call,
349: // but the Endpoint's build mutex is held.
350: func (p *EndpointPolicy) toMapState() {
351: 	p.L4Policy.Ingress.toMapState(p)
352: 	p.L4Policy.Egress.toMapState(p)
353: }
354: 
355: // toMapState transforms the L4DirectionPolicy into
356: // the datapath-friendly format inside EndpointPolicy.PolicyMapState.
357: // Called with selectorcache locked for reading.
358: // Called without holding the Repository lock.
359: // PolicyOwner (aka Endpoint) is also unlocked during this call,
360: // but the Endpoint's build mutex is held.
361: func (l4policy L4DirectionPolicy) toMapState(p *EndpointPolicy) {
362: 	l4policy.PortRules.ForEach(func(l4 *L4Filter) bool {
363: 		l4.toMapState(p, l4policy.features, ChangeState{})
364: 		return true
365: 	})
366: }
367: 
368: // RedirectFilters returns an iterator for each L4Filter with a redirect in the policy.
369: func (p *selectorPolicy) RedirectFilters() iter.Seq2[*L4Filter, *PerSelectorPolicy] {
370: 	return func(yield func(*L4Filter, *PerSelectorPolicy) bool) {
371: 		if p.L4Policy.Ingress.forEachRedirectFilter(yield) {
372: 			p.L4Policy.Egress.forEachRedirectFilter(yield)
373: 		}
374: 	}
375: }
376: 
377: func (l4policy L4DirectionPolicy) forEachRedirectFilter(yield func(*L4Filter, *PerSelectorPolicy) bool) bool {
378: 	ok := true
379: 	l4policy.PortRules.ForEach(func(l4 *L4Filter) bool {
380: 		if l4.IsRedirect() {
381: 			for _, ps := range l4.PerSelectorPolicies {
382: 				if ps != nil && ps.IsRedirect() {
383: 					ok = yield(l4, ps)
384: 				}
385: 			}
386: 		}
387: 		return ok
388: 	})
389: 	return ok
390: }
391: 
392: // ConsumeMapChanges applies accumulated MapChanges to EndpointPolicy 'p' and returns a symmary of changes.
393: // Caller is responsible for calling the returned 'closer' to release resources held for the new version!
394: // 'closer' may not be called while selector cache is locked!
395: func (p *EndpointPolicy) ConsumeMapChanges() (closer func(), changes ChangeState) {
396: 	features := p.selectorPolicy.L4Policy.Ingress.features | p.selectorPolicy.L4Policy.Egress.features
397: 	version, changes := p.policyMapChanges.consumeMapChanges(p, features)
398: 
399: 	closer = func() {}
400: 	if version.IsValid() {
401: 		var msg string
402: 		// update the version handle in p.VersionHandle so that any follow-on processing
403: 		// acts on the basis of the new version
404: 		if p.VersionHandle.IsValid() {
405: 			p.VersionHandle.Close()
406: 			msg = "ConsumeMapChanges: updated valid version"
407: 		} else {
408: 			closer = func() {
409: 				// p.VersionHandle was not valid, close it
410: 				p.Ready()
411: 			}
412: 			msg = "ConsumeMapChanges: new incremental version"
413: 		}
414: 		p.VersionHandle = version
415: 
416: 		p.PolicyOwner.PolicyDebug(logrus.Fields{
417: 			logfields.Version: version,
418: 			logfields.Changes: changes,
419: 		}, msg)
420: 	}
421: 
422: 	return closer, changes
423: }
424: 
425: // NewEndpointPolicy returns an empty EndpointPolicy stub.
426: func NewEndpointPolicy(repo PolicyRepository) *EndpointPolicy {
427: 	return &EndpointPolicy{
428: 		selectorPolicy: newSelectorPolicy(repo.GetSelectorCache()),
429: 		policyMapState: emptyMapState(),
430: 	}
431: }

55: 	// namespace

59: // EndpointFrontend is the interface to implement for an object to synchronize
60: // with the endpoint BPF map.
61: type EndpointFrontend interface {
62: 	LXCMac() mac.MAC
63: 	GetNodeMAC() mac.MAC
64: 	GetIfIndex() int
65: 	GetParentIfIndex() int
66: 	GetID() uint64
67: 	IPv4Address() netip.Addr
68: 	IPv6Address() netip.Addr
69: 	GetIdentity() identity.NumericIdentity
70: 	IsAtHostNS() bool
71: }
72: 
73: // GetBPFKeys returns all keys which should represent this endpoint in the BPF
74: // endpoints map
75: func GetBPFKeys(e EndpointFrontend) []*EndpointKey {
76: 	keys := []*EndpointKey{}
77: 	if e.IPv6Address().IsValid() {
78: 		keys = append(keys, NewEndpointKey(e.IPv6Address().AsSlice()))
79: 	}

585: 	return nil

steps.message:
I am thinking...: I now have enough information to write a comprehensive answer to all four questions. Let me organize what I've found:

**Q1: Per-Node eBPF Lifecycle**
- The `loader` struct in `pkg/datapath/loader/loader.go` is the central component responsible for compiling, loading, and attaching eBPF programs on each node.
- Compilation happens via `compile()` in `pkg/datapath/loader/compile.go`, which invokes `clang` with node-specific flags:
  - `getBPFCPU()` probes the local kernel for ISA support (v1/v2/v3) using `probes.HaveV3ISA()`, `probes.HaveV2ISA()` - this is node-specific
  - `-D__NR_CPUS__` is set from `ebpf.PossibleCPU()` - node-specific
  - Include paths from `option.Config.BpfDir` and `option.Config.StateDir` contain node-specific headers
- The `objectCache` in `pkg/datapath/loader/cache.go` caches compiled templates per-node. `fetchOrCompile()` hashes the endpoint configuration and node config to determine if recompilation is needed.
- `ReloadDatapath()` in `loader.go` is the main entry point: it calls `templateCache.fetchOrCompile()` then `reloadEndpoint()` or `reloadHostEndpoint()`.
- The eBPF program becomes node-local at the point of `bpf.LoadAndAssign()` in `reloadEndpoint()` - this loads the ELF into the kernel via the local kernel's BPF syscall.
- Node-specific configuration is written via `writeNodeConfigHeader()` (writes `node_config.h`) and `writeNetdevHeader()` (writes `netdev_config.h`).

**Q2: Deployment Architecture**
- Cilium is deployed as a DaemonSet (confirmed in `Documentation/network/kubernetes/concepts.rst` lines 19-23: "A DaemonSet resource: describes the Cilium pod that is deployed to each Kubernetes node. This pod runs the cilium-agent and associated daemons.")
- Each node runs its own independent `cilium-agent` process with its own `loader` instance, `objectCache`, and `compilationLock`.
- When one node's agent fails to initialize eBPF programs, the failure is contained to that node. The `startRegenerationFailureHandler()` in `pkg/endpoint/policy.go` (lines 908-944) creates a controller that retries regeneration on failure - this is per-endpoint, per-node.
- The control plane (Kubernetes API server, etcd) distributes policy CRDs cluster-wide, but the data plane (eBPF programs, maps) is entirely local to each node.
- The `compilationLock` in `loader.go` is a node-local mutex that serializes base program compilation with endpoint compilation on that node only.
- `hostDpInitializedOnce` and `hostDpInitialized` channel in `loader.go` (lines 81-82) signal when the host datapath is ready on the local node.

**Q3: Policy Distribution vs. Enforcement**
- CiliumNetworkPolicy (CNP) resources are cluster-wide Kubernetes CRDs.
- Each node's agent watches these CRDs via `pkg/policy/k8s/cell.go` - the `startK8sPolicyWatcher()` function starts a per-node watcher that translates CNP/CCNP/KNP resources into Cilium's internal policy representation.
- The `policyWatcher` in `pkg/policy/k8s/cilium_network_policy.go` calls `p.policyImporter.UpdatePolicy()` to update the local policy repository.
- The local `Repository` in `pkg/policy/repository.go` stores the policy rules in-memory on each node.
- The `policyCache` in `pkg/policy/distillery.go` resolves policies per-identity using `updateSelectorPolicy()` → `cache.repo.resolvePolicyLocked()`.
- `selectorPolicy.DistillPolicy()` in `pkg/policy/resolve.go` converts the selector-based policy into concrete `EndpointPolicy` with a `policyMapState`.
- The `Endpoint.regenerateBPF()` in `pkg/endpoint/bpf.go` calls `realizeBPFState()` which calls `e.owner.Orchestrator().ReloadDatapath()` - this is the point where policy becomes eBPF bytecode on the local node.
- A compilation failure on one node returns an error from `ReloadDatapath()` (line 566-571 in bpf.go), which is logged and the endpoint is marked as failed. The K8s watcher on other nodes continues independently - they have their own policy repositories and their own compilation pipelines.
- `CiliumNetworkPolicyNodeStatus` in `pkg/k8s/apis/cilium.io/v2/cnp_types.go` tracks per-node policy status - each node reports its own status independently.

**Q4: eBPF Map Scoping and State Isolation**
- eBPF maps are node-local. They are pinned to the BPF filesystem (bpffs) on each node.
- `bpf.TCGlobalsPath()` returns `<bpffs>/tc/globals` (typically `/sys/fs/bpf/tc/globals`) - this is the host's local bpffs.
- `bpf.CiliumPath()` returns `<bpffs>/cilium` - also local.
- Per-endpoint policy maps are named `cilium_policy_v2_<endpoint-id>` (from `policymap.MapName = "cilium_policy_v2_"` in `pkg/maps/policymap/policymap.go`).
- `policymap.OpenOrCreate(path)` creates/opens the map at a local path like `/sys/fs/bpf/tc/globals/cilium_policy_v2_00001`.
- `bpf.LocalMapPath(name, id)` in `pkg/bpf/bpffs_linux.go` constructs the path as `<TCGlobalsPath>/<name><id>` - entirely local.
- Per-endpoint bpffs directories: `bpffsEndpointDir()` returns `/sys/fs/bpf/cilium/endpoints/<endpoint-id>` (from `pkg/datapath/loader/paths.go`).
- The `lxcmap` (`cilium_lxc`) is a node-local hash map that maps endpoint IPs to endpoint info.
- If a node fails to create or update a map, the error is returned from `lxcmap.WriteEndpoint()` (line 473 in bpf.go) or `policymap.OpenOrCreate()`, causing only that endpoint's regeneration to fail on that node. Other nodes' maps are completely independent - they live in different kernel instances.
- The BPF filesystem is mounted per-node (each node has its own `/sys/fs/bpf`), so there is no shared state between nodes at the map level.
- Global maps like `cilium_call_policy` (PolicyCallMapName) and `cilium_egresscall_policy` (PolicyEgressCallMapName) are also node-local - they exist in each node's kernel.
